purpose: Friction closure mdot comparison — F1/F3_hagenbach/F3_shah_apparent/F4_leg_class on Salt 2/3/4 Jin at matched insulation
scope: Salt 2 (ins=0.25 in), Salt 3 (ins=0.30 in), Salt 4 (ins=0.30 in) Jin coarse-mesh cases; 4 friction forms; 12 solver runs total

changes_to_existing.change: Docstring fix in dp_F3_hagenbach() lines 206-208: corrected 'CONSERVATIVE (over-estimates)' to 'underestimates total entry-length ΔP at x+ < 1 because it omits the elevated distributed wall shear'

open_questions_for_user: F4_leg_class over-predicts loop friction by ~24% on all salts — should TODO-RI-CORRECTED-F4 be promoted to HIGH priority?
F3_shah_apparent has residual +3-4% error at Salt 3/4; is this within acceptable tolerance for the 1D model or should insulation sensitivity be explored?
Should the mdot_comparison.csv serve as input to TODO-MODEL-FORM-BAKEOFF directly, or does it need the observation-table contract structure first?
